frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Aurat ki asli izzat tab samajh aati hai jab aadmi usme kisi ki maa, beti ya bahu dekhe. Hamare yahan aurat ko isliye sar aankhon par rakhte hain kyunki woh ghar ko janam deti hai, sambhalti hai, warna aaj kal to log bas individual bolkar sab seema hi bhool gaye hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it defines women’s worth through family roles like mother, daughter, and daughter-in-law rather than as individuals. It uses relational framing and conditional respect that sounds respectful on the surface while erasing women’s independent personhood.
original_frame: Religious and Caste Identity as Dress Mandate: Her Body as Community Property
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 52. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in a village in UP. Your son recently married a girl named Priya who is from the same caste but from a city family. Priya does not cover her head Ñ she does not wear a ghoonghat and has never been asked to in her own family. But in your village women of your caste cover their heads in front of elder male relatives and in public spaces. This is tradition. Priya has been here three months and has not adopted the ghoonghat. Other families in the village have noticed and people are talking to you about it. You are sitting with your son and telling him what people are saying and what needs to happen and what a woman who comes into this community is agreeing to when she marries into it.
